title: Review Implement External Guardrail Templates And Prompt Loading

handoff_summary.completed: Reviewed `src/codex_orchestrator/prompts.py`, `templates/agents/*.md`, `tests/test_orchestrator.py`, and the linked spec update. Verified that prompt generation now injects external guardrails, missing templates raise a clear `FileNotFoundError`, docs describe the narrowed B0003 slice, and the current test suite passes via `PYTHONPATH=.orchestrator/worktrees/B0002/src python -m unittest discover -s .orchestrator/worktrees/B0002/tests -p 'test_orchestrator.py'` (25 tests).
handoff_summary.remaining: A developer follow-up is needed to package or otherwise reliably ship `templates/agents/*.md` with the distribution and add a regression test that exercises template loading from an installed/package-like layout. Until then, this feature is only reliable when run directly from a checkout that already contains the templates beside the source tree.
handoff_summary.risks: High confidence runtime risk for packaged installs: `TEMPLATES_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / "templates" / "agents"` in `src/codex_orchestrator/prompts.py` assumes repository-style layout, while `pyproject.toml` currently declares only Python packages and no package data. Current tests also read the real checkout templates rather than templates under the temporary test repository, so they would not catch a packaging/distribution failure.
handoff_summary.next_action: Fix template distribution so the required guardrail files are available outside an in-repo checkout, then add a regression test for that packaging/runtime path and rerun the orchestrator tests.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Low for follow-up if scope stays on `pyproject.toml`, packaging metadata, and prompt/template-loading tests. Coordinate if another bead is already editing `src/codex_orchestrator/prompts.py`, `tests/test_orchestrator.py`, or `templates/agents/*.md`.

conflict_risks: Low for follow-up if scope stays on `pyproject.toml`, packaging metadata, and prompt/template-loading tests. Coordinate if another bead is already editing `src/codex_orchestrator/prompts.py`, `tests/test_orchestrator.py`, or `templates/agents/*.md`.

execution_history.summary: Review found one blocking-quality issue in the guardrail-template slice: the new `templates/agents/*.md` files are loaded from the source tree but are not included in packaging metadata, so installed CLI/runtime environments will fail to find required templates even though tests pass in-repo.
